notes: Wrapped in an object (not a bare array) so the file carries the top-level synthetic attestation, mirroring sitrep.replay.load_scenario, which rejects bare lists (F13/INV-J). Cite ref formats: clause -> a clause id in the referenced pack; note -> 'note:<start>:<end>' character span into case clinical_note; transcript -> 'transcript:<turn>' 1-based turn index. pack_hash values are sha256 over the pack file bytes, computed at authoring time; regenerate if a pack changes.

fixtures.purpose: Appeal contains a clinical claim with empty cites (and unsupported by the record). An unsupported clinical assertion must BLOCK: a claim no reviewer can trace is a fabrication risk regardless of how plausible it sounds.

claims.text: Standardized testing (PLS-5) documented Expressive Communication below the 5th percentile for age.
The patient has already failed six months of private speech therapy with a different provider.

fixtures.purpose: Determination cites an authority id that does not exist in the pack (and is proprietary-criteria-shaped). Citation integrity must BLOCK even when the outcome is 'approve' — fabricated authorities are never shippable in either direction.

claims.text: Approval duration is limited to 12 weeks pending re-evaluation.

fixtures.purpose: Determination attempts outcome 'deny' while its own first claim establishes the evidence is indeterminate (score report not attached, element unverifiable). Fail-closed: indeterminate evidence routes to ESCALATE (physician review plus a records request) — never a deny. Physician sign-off is present to isolate indeterminacy as the only defect.

claims.text: The submitted record references PLS-5 administration, but the score report is not attached; the standardized-assessment element cannot be verified from the documents provided.
Functional impact is documented in the clinical note.

fixtures.purpose: Attempted machine auto-deny: outcome 'deny' with physician_signoff null. A denial is an adverse benefit determination — it must BLOCK without physician sign-off, no matter how complete the citations look. No override parameter exists by design.

claims.text: Documentation does not establish that skilled services, rather than continuation of the caregiver-implemented home program, are required.

fixtures.purpose: Happy path: every claim carries at least one resolvable cite (real clause id, in-range note span, or transcript turn), all cited authorities exist in the pack, and provenance is complete. Expected ALLOW (as a DRAFT for physician review — never auto-filed).

claims.text: A standardized, norm-referenced assessment (PLS-5), administered by a licensed speech-language pathologist, documented Expressive Communication below the 5th percentile for age.
The delay has a documented functional impact: the patient cannot verbally request basic needs, and failed communication attempts end in tantrums several times per day.

cites.quote: Every day, sometimes three or four times. Yesterday he threw himself on the floor because he wanted a specific cup and couldn't tell me which one.

claims.text: A caregiver-implemented home program has been carried out consistently for approximately one year with only minimal gain; direct skilled services by a licensed speech-language pathologist are therefore required.

cites.quote: Yes, and we do them every day. We narrate everything, we offer choices, we hold things up and wait for him to try the word, we read every night. We've done it for almost a year and he's gained only a handful of words.

claims.text: The plan of care states discipline and frequency (2 sessions per week), measurable goals with target criteria, and a 12-week re-evaluation interval.
Hearing was screened and is normal, excluding hearing loss as the primary cause of the delay.

cites.quote: We had it checked. The hearing screen at the pediatrician's office was normal — he passed in both ears.

claims.text: The patient is under 21 and Medicaid-enrolled, so the EPSDT correct-or-ameliorate standard governs; the denial's unspecified 'does not meet criteria' rationale cannot displace individual EPSDT medical-necessity review.
